Summary
Experienced Hadoop Developer with over 2 years of professional experience in big data ecosystems, skilled in data ware-
housing, ingestion, processing, and optimization. Proficient in Apache Hadoop, Hive, Sqoop, Flume, Python, and SQL,
with a strong background in developing robust data pipelines and performing cluster health management.

Experience
Hadoop Developer Full-time
Tata Consultancy Services June 2015 – Present
•Provided engineering support for analytics and application teams within the Data Lake program, focusing on
Hadoop cluster setup, governance, and smooth functioning.
•Developed a proactive Health Check utility for Data Lake, ensuring continuous monitoring of Hadoop components
and automating HTML email reports for daily checks and post-upgrade validations.

•Conducted benchmark testing on Hive and Impala queries to assess and improve query performance across the

tation for essential services like Hive Server2, Impala, and HDFS.
•Filtered and refined data using advanced technologies such as Pig and Regex Serde, ensuring data relevance and
quality for analysis.
•Executed Dataiku benchmark testing against Impala, Hive, and Greenplum database to evaluate performance differ-
entials in data processing.
•Migrated data from Greenplum database to Hadoop via Sqoop pipelines, processing and storing it in Hive tables for
subsequent performance testing.
•Addressed and resolved Hadoop ecosystem-related issues, ensuring the stability and reliability of the WM Hadoop
ecosystem.
•Rescheduled jobs from Autosys to TWS job hosting to achieve better performance and optimize job execution sched-
ules.
Project: ABSA- Reconciliations, UBC and WMDATALAKE COE Clients: Barclays, Union Bank of California, Morgan Stanley
Tata Consultancy Services Within above

tenure •Led the migration of data from RDBMS to Hadoop (Hive) to enhance data governance, lineage, auditing, and moni-
toring capabilities, addressing limitations of RDBMS in processing vast amounts of data.
•Optimized SQL queries for data that did not require migration, ensuring efficient handling of existing database
assets.
•Developed complex Hive queries and logic to facilitate seamless data movement from RDBMS to the Hadoop ecosys-
tem.
•Wrote Hive queries to analyze required data according to specific business requirements, aiding in rapid analysis
and decision-making.

•Further optimized Hive queries to maximize performance and minimize Hadoop resource utilization, critical for
large-scale data processing.
•Created and implemented Sqoop commands and scripts for efficient data transfer from RDBMS to the Hadoop
platform.
Project: Intellimatch Reconciliation Migration Role: Developer

functionality.

Eslam Kamal is a Hadoop Developer with over 2 years of experience, proficient in technologies like Apache Hadoop, Hive, Sqoop, Flume, Python, and SQL. He can be contacted via eslam.kamal@email.com, +20 100 123 4567, github.com/eslam-kamal, and linkedin.com/in/eslam-kamal, and has a personal website eslamkamal.com.
Eslam Kamal received a B.E. in Electronics & Communication from Medi-caps Institute of Technology & Management in Indore, Madhya Pradesh, India, graduating between May 2011 and May 2015.

As a Hadoop Developer at Tata Consultancy Services from June 2015 to the present, Eslam Kamal provided engineering support for the Data Lake program, focusing on Hadoop cluster setup and governance. He developed a Health Check utility for the Data Lake, automated reports, designed data pipelines using Sqoop, optimized Hive queries, and configured Flume agents.

Eslam Kamal developed a proactive Health Check utility for the Data Lake, automating HTML email reports for daily checks and post-upgrade validations. He also automated cluster health, usage, and capacity reports using Unix shell scripting, and conducted benchmark testing on Hive and Impala queries.

Eslam Kamal implemented data pipelines to ingest data from RDBMS to Hive external tables using Sqoop, incorporating data quality checks like row count validation. He also filtered and refined data using Pig and Regex Serde, and migrated data from Greenplum to Hadoop via Sqoop.

Eslam Kamal addressed Hadoop ecosystem issues, rescheduled jobs from Autosys to TWS, and performed benchmark testing against Impala, Hive, and Greenplum. He worked on projects including ABSA- Reconciliations, UBC, and WMDATALAKE for clients like Barclays, Union Bank of California, and Morgan Stanley.

The individual led the migration of data from RDBMS to Hadoop (Hive) to improve data governance, lineage, auditing, and monitoring, addressing the limitations of RDBMS for large datasets. They also optimized existing SQL queries and developed complex Hive queries to facilitate data movement and analysis.
The individual optimized Hive queries for performance and resource utilization, and created Sqoop commands and scripts for efficient data transfer from RDBMS to Hadoop.

As a Developer at Tata Consultancy Services for the Intellimatch Reconciliation Migration project, the individual extracted and implemented business requirements, migrated static setups from Intellimatch version 8.1 to 9.1, and performed backend development using SQL queries.
The individual completed the Initial Learning Program at Tata Consultancy Services from June 2015 to August 2015.

The individual possesses skills in various programming languages including Apache Hadoop, Python, Shell Scripting, SQL, and Scala, as well as Big Data Technologies like Hive, Pig, Sqoop, and tools such as Dataiku, Cloudera, and Eclipse.
The individual is proficient in operating systems like Unix.

Hive is a cornerstone of modern data warehousing, a vital component within the broader Hadoop ecosystem. It presents data stored in Hadoop as tables, rows, and columns – familiar territory for SQL users – enabling querying and analysis of massive datasets with relative ease. More than just a query engine, Hive is instrumental in establishing robust data governance, meticulously tracking data lineage, and implementing comprehensive auditing and monitoring procedures. 

It’s a workhorse for those grappling with datasets too large for traditional relational database management systems, offering a scalable solution for data movement and analysis. Skilled developers, like Eslam Kamal, dedicate themselves to unlocking Hive’s full potential, optimizing queries for peak performance and efficient resource utilization. Kamal’s expertise extends to crafting complex Hive queries and streamlining data transfer from relational databases using Sqoop, effectively migrating data *to* Hive to overcome the limitations of older systems. 

Hive isn’t merely a tool; it’s a facilitator of business requirements, providing the foundation for informed decision-making. It’s a key player in building data lakes, and its capabilities are frequently benchmarked against other technologies like Impala and Greenplum to ensure optimal performance. Ultimately, Hive empowers organizations to harness the power of big data, transforming raw information into actionable insights.

Sqoop is a vital conduit in the world of Big Data, a tool forged for the practical task of bridging the gap between structured data residing in relational database management systems (RDBMS) and the expansive, unstructured world of Hadoop. It’s not a flashy component, but a workhorse – reliable, efficient, and essential for populating data lakes and enabling comprehensive analytics. 

Think of Sqoop as a skilled translator, fluent in both SQL and the languages of Hadoop – MapReduce, Hive, and others. It doesn’t simply copy data; it intelligently transfers it, capable of filtering, transforming, and even incorporating basic data quality checks during the process.  Eslam Kamal, a Hadoop Developer at Tata Consultancy Services, understands this intimately, having wielded Sqoop to build robust data pipelines. He’s used it to ingest data from databases directly into Hive external tables, ensuring data integrity with row count validations, and even orchestrated complex migrations, like moving data from Greenplum to Hadoop. 

Kamal’s work highlights Sqoop’s core strength: its ability to automate the often-tedious process of data transfer. He crafts Sqoop commands and scripts, optimizing them for speed and efficiency, turning what could be a manual, error-prone task into a streamlined operation.  It’s a tool favored by those who build and maintain Hadoop ecosystems, a cornerstone technology for anyone seeking to unlock the value hidden within traditional databases.

SQL is a foundational language, a workhorse of data manipulation and definition. Not flashy, but relentlessly practical, it’s the tool Eslam Kamal, a Hadoop Developer, consistently returns to – a testament to its enduring power. While Kamal navigates the complexities of Big Data ecosystems like Hadoop, Hive, and Spark, SQL remains central to his toolkit, used for both optimization of traditional relational database queries *and* the construction of intricate queries within the Hive environment. 

It’s a language of precision, demanded during Kamal’s work at Tata Consultancy Services on the Intellimatch Reconciliation Migration project, where it was employed for backend development and the meticulous implementation of business requirements. SQL isn’t merely *used* by Kamal; it’s actively shaped by him, refined to move and analyze data, bridging the gap between older systems and newer, scalable architectures. 

Though often overshadowed by newer programming languages, SQL’s strength lies in its reliability and universality. It’s a core skill, a fundamental building block for anyone working with data, and a key component in Kamal’s ability to improve data governance, lineage, and auditing – proving that sometimes, the most powerful tools are the ones that have stood the test of time. It’s a language that doesn’t boast, but *delivers*.

Eslam Kamal is a resourceful Hadoop Developer with over two years dedicated to building and maintaining robust data solutions. A 2015 graduate of Medi-caps Institute of Technology & Management, where he earned a B.E. in Electronics & Communication, he quickly found his niche within the world of big data. Currently at Tata Consultancy Services, Eslam is a key contributor to Data Lake programs, specializing in cluster setup, governance, and performance optimization. 

He’s a practical engineer, evidenced by his development of a proactive Health Check utility – a system automating daily and post-upgrade validations with clear HTML reports – and automated reporting using Unix shell scripting. Eslam isn’t content with simply maintaining systems; he actively improves them, optimizing Hive queries and configuring Flume agents for efficient data flow. 

His skillset is broad, encompassing Apache Hadoop, Hive, Sqoop, Flume, Python, and SQL. He’s adept at designing data pipelines, seamlessly ingesting data from RDBMS systems into Hive using Sqoop, complete with rigorous data quality checks. He’s also experienced in data migration, having successfully moved data from Greenplum to Hadoop. 

Eslam’s work impacts major financial institutions, having contributed to projects like ABSA-Reconciliations, UBC, and WMDATALAKE for clients including Barclays, Union Bank of California, and Morgan Stanley. He’s readily reachable via eslam.kamal@email.com, +20 100 123 4567, and maintains a professional presence on Github (github.com/eslam-kamal) and LinkedIn (linkedin.com/in/eslam-kamal), alongside his personal website (eslamkamal.com).

Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) is a global engine of technical expertise, a launching pad for careers and a key player in data solutions and system migrations. More than just an employer, TCS functions as a comprehensive training ground, evidenced by its robust Initial Learning Program – a foundational experience for new hires. The company’s strength lies in its ability to tackle complex projects, as demonstrated through initiatives like the Data Lake program, where TCS supports the entire lifecycle from cluster setup and governance to the creation of vital monitoring tools. Developers within TCS, like Eslam Kamal, are instrumental in building and maintaining these systems, crafting automated reports, designing efficient data pipelines with tools like Sqoop and Flume, and optimizing query performance with Hive. TCS also excels in modernization efforts, seamlessly migrating legacy systems – such as the Intellimatch Reconciliation platform from version 8.1 to 9.1 – while ensuring business requirements are meticulously translated into functional backend solutions using SQL. In essence, TCS is a dynamic organization focused on delivering robust, scalable, and future-proof technology solutions, nurturing talent, and driving digital transformation for its clients.
